LEGO Organization Plan

Interactive map for the black and red 44-drawer cabinets

Similar shapes and sizes were grouped first, color was used as a secondary ordering cue inside each zone, and each drawer’s “load” was balanced with a geometry-based plastic-volume proxy so the small and large drawers stay reasonably comparable relative to their size. A few outlier drawers are intentionally lighter or heavier because each part/color lot stays intact in one drawer.

Total pieces
6,181
Unique lots
526
Small drawers
64
Large drawers
24
Cabinets
2
Rows guide
A–G
Quick row guide A: Tiles B: Plates C: Plate specials + slopes + some bricks D: Bricks + brackets + odd small parts E–F: Bulk flat parts G: Oversize plates + large bricks + wedges

Black cabinet

Rows A–D small drawers · Rows E–G large drawers

Red cabinet

Rows A–D small drawers · Rows E–G large drawers

Select any drawer to inspect its assigned parts. Clicking a part in the index will also jump back here and highlight the drawer.

526 lots
Load percentages are based on an estimated plastic-volume proxy rather than exact measured gram weights. That keeps the drawer plan practical and consistent, but a few single-lot outliers—especially very large plates—still land heavier or lighter than average because the whole lot stays together in one drawer.

Drop image to find parts

Instruction manuals or photos · ⌘V to paste

Reset inventory?

This clears all saved complete, partial, and missing checks from this browser. It will not change the drawer plan or filters.
